name: White Chocolate Chip with Macadamia Nut
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease two cookie sheets lightly.
In a food processor with steel blade, blend butter, sugars, egg, and vanilla until fluffy. Scrape the sides of the bowl once, about 1 minute total.
Add flour, baking soda, and salt; mix until just combined using 2 or 3 pulses. Do not over-process.
Transfer to a large bowl and stir in chocolate and nuts.
Mound dough by 1/3 cupfuls onto prepared cookie sheets, spacing about 2 inches apart.
Bake for about 15 minutes or until light brown around the edges. Cool on the cookie sheets for 3 minutes before transferring to a rack to cool completely.
Next, prepare hot fudge sauce:
Combine cream and sugar in a heavy saucepan over low heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Add chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Stir in butter and corn syrup until butter melts.
Serve warm with the white chocolate chip macadamia nut brownies.
